title: Moist Flaky Malaysian Sea Bass Satay
description: A traditional Malaysian dish featuring sea bass fillets, this recipe combines the flavors of Southeast Asia with the simplicity of satay, making it a show-off dish for any occasion. The resinous and flaky texture of the sea bass is balanced by the ayurvedic-tamasic friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, and salt.
Add the sea bass fillets to the marinade and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Thread the marinated sea bass fillets onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the skewers with peanut oil and grill for 4-5 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Serve the satay hot with your choice of sides, such as steamed rice or roasted vegetables.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and lime wedges, if desired.
Let the satay rest for a few minutes before serving.
